title: Build hierarchical out-of-fold strategy priors with empirical-Bayes shrinkage
summary: Weekend strategy is strongly race-specific: some events are naturally one-stop, others two-stop or high-undercut.
rationale: Weekend strategy is strongly race-specific: some events are naturally one-stop, others two-stop or high-undercut. Instead of generic target encoding, a hierarchical prior can estimate stop propensity for cells like `(race-year, absolute_compound, stint, binned_laps_remaining, wet/dry)` and shrink sparse cells toward broader parents. That matches how public strategy guides discuss races in terms of circuit-level priors and compound context.
implementation_hint: Create nested OOF priors such as `Race_Year+AbsCompound+Stint+LapBin`, then back off to `Race_Year+AbsCompound+Stint`, `Race_Year+AbsCompound`, `AbsCompound+Stint`, and global mean. Use additive smoothing or Bayesian shrinkage only from training folds; expose both the posterior mean and the gap to parent/global priors as features. This is especially useful after reconstructing absolute compound IDs.
expected_effect: Should add strong weekend-context signal without hand-coding thresholds, while staying leak-safe if computed fully OOF.
risk: Easy to leak if priors are computed on full data; over-binning can create noise and under-binning can wash out signal.
